benchmark_description: Partition a rectangle into rows and columns of same-size squares and count to find the total number of squares.

lesson.title: Partitioning Rectangles into Equal Squares
lesson.objective: Students will divide rectangles into equal rows and columns of squares and count the total squares.

vocabulary.definition: To divide a shape into equal parts.
Squares going across (left to right) in a rectangle.
Squares going up and down in a rectangle.
Squares that are all equal — the same height and width.
The whole amount; all parts added together.

lesson.introduction: When you divide a rectangle into rows and columns of same-size squares, you make an array — like a grid. Counting these squares connects geometry to multiplication! A rectangle cut into 3 rows and 4 columns has 3 × 4 = 12 squares.

concepts.heading: Rows and Columns
concepts.body: Rows go across (horizontally). Columns go up and down (vertically). If a rectangle has 2 rows and 5 columns, there are 2 × 5 = 10 squares. You can also count them one by one, but adding or multiplying is faster.

concepts.heading: How to Count the Squares
concepts.body: Method 1: Count each square one by one. Method 2: Count one row, then add that many for each row. Example: 3 rows, 4 in each row → 4 + 4 + 4 = 12 squares. Method 3: rows × columns = total.

concepts.heading: Drawing the Partition
concepts.body: To partition a rectangle into 2 rows and 3 columns: draw 1 line across the middle (makes 2 rows) and 2 lines down (makes 3 columns). Count the squares you made: 6 total.

worked_example.problem: A rectangle is divided into 4 rows and 3 columns of equal squares. How many squares are there in all?
steps: Look at the rows: there are 4 rows.
Look at the columns: there are 3 columns.
Add one row at a time: 3 + 3 + 3 + 3 = 12.

lesson.summary: To find the total squares in a partitioned rectangle, count the rows and columns. Add the number of squares in each row, or use rows × columns.

practice_questions.question: A rectangle is divided into 2 rows and 2 columns. How many equal squares are there?
practice_questions.explanation: 2 rows × 2 columns = 4 squares. Imagine a square divided into 4 smaller equal squares.

practice_questions.question: A rectangle is divided into 1 row and 5 columns. How many squares are there?
practice_questions.explanation: 1 row with 5 columns = 5 squares. Just one row of 5 side-by-side squares.

practice_questions.question: A rectangle is divided into 3 rows and 4 columns of equal squares. How many squares are there in all?
A rectangle has 2 rows. Each row has 6 squares. How many squares are there in all?
practice_questions.explanation: Each row has 6 squares and there are 2 rows: 6 + 6 = 12 squares in all.

practice_questions.question: A rectangle is divided into 5 rows and 2 columns. How many squares?
A rectangle is partitioned into 4 rows and 5 columns of equal squares. How many squares are there?
A grid has 24 equal squares in 4 rows. How many columns does it have?
practice_questions.explanation: If there are 24 squares in 4 rows, each row has 24 ÷ 4 = 6 squares. So there are 6 columns.

practice_questions.question: Tom drew a rectangle divided into 3 rows and 6 columns. Maria drew one with 6 rows and 3 columns. Who has more squares?
practice_questions.explanation: Tom: 3 × 6 = 18. Maria: 6 × 3 = 18. Both have 18 squares. The total is the same even when rows and columns are swapped.

practice_questions.question: A large rectangle is divided into 5 rows and 5 columns of equal squares. How many squares are there?
practice_questions.explanation: 5 × 5 = 25 squares. Each row has 5, and there are 5 rows: 5 + 5 + 5 + 5 + 5 = 25.

practice_questions.question: A rectangle is divided into equal squares. It has 3 rows. There are 18 squares in all. How many squares are in each row?
practice_questions.explanation: 18 total squares ÷ 3 rows = 6 squares in each row.

mastery_check.question: A rectangle is partitioned into 3 rows and 7 columns of equal squares. How many squares are there in all?
A rectangle has 30 equal squares arranged in 5 rows. How many columns does it have?
Ella divided a rectangle into equal squares with 4 rows. Each row had 8 squares. Ben divided a rectangle into 6 rows with 5 squares each. Who has more squares?
mastery_check.explanation: Ella: 4 × 8 = 32. Ben: 6 × 5 = 30. Ella has 2 more squares.

remediation.question: A rectangle is divided into 1 row and 3 columns. How many squares are there?
remediation.explanation: One row with 3 squares in it = 3 squares total.

remediation.question: Squares that go across (left to right) in a rectangle make a ___.
remediation.explanation: A row goes left to right (across). A column goes up and down.

remediation.question: A rectangle has 2 rows and 3 columns of equal squares. How many squares are there?
remediation.explanation: 2 rows with 3 in each: 3 + 3 = 6 squares. Or 2 × 3 = 6.

remediation.question: A rectangle is divided into 3 rows and 3 columns. How many squares?
remediation.explanation: 3 rows × 3 columns = 9 squares. Like a tic-tac-toe grid — it has 9 boxes.

remediation.question: A rectangle has 4 rows. Each row has 2 squares. How many squares in all?
